title: The Gang Wrestles for the Troops

jokes.text: What's going on with Hulk Hogan's hair? It's blond and yet it's silken, like that of a Chinese man.
jokes.setup: Character notices something odd about Hulk Hogan's appearance
jokes.punchline: Comparing his hair texture to 'that of a Chinese man' - weirdly specific and absurd
jokes.explanation: Absurd specificity works well - the comparison is so random it's funny

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: What's going on with Hulk Hogan's hair? It's blond and yet it's silken, like that of a Chinese man.

jokes.text: Blonde, Chinese hair... And skin of a hot dog.
jokes.setup: Building on the Hulk Hogan description
jokes.punchline: Hot dog skin - escalates the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that takes the absurdity further with vivid imagery

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Blonde, Chinese hair... And skin of a hot dog.

jokes.text: Hey sexy! I can't get you out of my mind.
jokes.punchline: Awkward flirtatious message delivery
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe moment, works for character but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Hey sexy! I can't get you out of my mind.

jokes.text: So that would make you Desert Rose. - Yes it would. - [Snort] Wow! What a couple of losers!
jokes.punchline: Immediate mockery from the gang
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the gang's immediate cruelty to each other

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: So that would make you Desert Rose.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yes it would.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Wow! What a couple of losers!

jokes.text: High suicide rates for this guy. He's going to be crushed.
jokes.setup: Discussing the soldier finding out about Dee's deception
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning suicide as an inevitable outcome
jokes.explanation: Classic dark Sunny humor - Charlie's matter-of-fact delivery of something horrible

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: High suicide rates for this guy. He's going to be crushed.

jokes.text: And when Hogan was doing his thing, and we were killing Iranians, or whatever... I mean we were proud!
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning killing people as part of patriotic nostalgia
jokes.explanation: Mac's oblivious nationalism taken to dark extremes

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: And when Hogan was doing his thing, and we were killing Iranians, or whatever... I mean we were proud!

jokes.text: Wrestling.
jokes.setup: What's the best way to celebrate America?
jokes.punchline: Simple, confident answer: Wrestling
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character consistency - Mac's simple logic

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Wrestling.

jokes.text: That's right. We're the Pigeon Boys! Pigeon Boys!
jokes.setup: Revealing their old wrestling team name
jokes.punchline: Enthusiasm for the objectively uncool 'Pigeon Boys'
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between their enthusiasm and how pathetic it sounds

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: That's right. We're the Pigeon Boys! Pigeon Boys!

jokes.text: Doesn't sound that cool now as an adult. You're right.
jokes.punchline: Mac's immediate deflation and agreement
jokes.explanation: Good deflation moment, Mac's quick retreat from his own idea

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Doesn't sound that cool now as an adult. You're right.

jokes.text: I don't know. I feel like pigeons are survivors though.
jokes.setup: Discussion about changing from Pigeon Boys
jokes.punchline: Charlie's earnest defense of pigeons as survivors
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - finding noble qualities in gross things

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I don't know. I feel like pigeons are survivors though.

jokes.text: This is uh... Parking ticket. You betcha. You have fun with it. Okay?
jokes.setup: Maniac offering what seems like an autograph
jokes.punchline: It's just a parking ticket he's giving them
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of expectations - perfectly establishes Maniac's mental state

dialogue.speaker: The Maniac
dialogue.line: This is uh... Parking ticket. You betcha. You have fun with it. Okay?

jokes.text: $30 bucks, weekdays. $50 bucks, weekends...
jokes.punchline: Extremely low rates delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Shows how low-rent this operation is while establishing Maniac's professionalism about his terrible career

dialogue.speaker: The Maniac
dialogue.line: $30 bucks, weekdays. $50 bucks, weekends...

jokes.text: Well, my chat name is Desert Rose, so I dressed like a rose, I'm going to give him a rose, and I'm going to play Kiss From a Rose by Seal.
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top literal interpretation of her screen name
jokes.explanation: Classic Dee overcomplication, shows her shallow understanding of romance

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Well, my chat name is Desert Rose, so I dressed like a rose, I'm going to give him a rose, and I'm going to play Kiss From a Rose by Seal.

jokes.text: You bought him a pair of jean shorts?
jokes.punchline: Incredulous reaction to the inappropriate gift choice
jokes.explanation: Jean shorts are inherently funny, especially as a military homecoming gift

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You bought him a pair of jean shorts?

jokes.text: Great girl. Uh. She just wanted me to come here so that I could tell you. That she wasn't going to be able to show up here. Today.
jokes.setup: Soldier expecting to meet Desert Rose
jokes.punchline: Awkward improvised excuse delivered poorly
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe moment, not particularly clever but fits character

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Great girl. Uh. She just wanted me to come here so that I could tell you. That she wasn't going to be able to show up here. Today.

jokes.text: You thought it because of this and the. It's fine. Don't blame yourself. You're not, you're not dumb. You're just.
jokes.setup: Soldier assumes she's Desert Rose because of her costume
jokes.punchline: Dee's condescending reassurance while clearly lying
jokes.explanation: Dee's terrible attempt at damage control while patronizing the victim of her deception

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You thought it because of this and the. It's fine. Don't blame yourself. You're not, you're not dumb. You're just.

jokes.text: I don't give a rat's ass about your collection agency. And as for the $15 co-payment.... Eat shit and die!
jokes.punchline: Extreme aggression over a tiny amount of money
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Maniac's unhinged nature over trivial matters

dialogue.line: I don't give a rat's ass about your collection agency. And as for the $15 co-payment.... Eat shit and die!

jokes.text: That's my character, I'm the Trash Man. I come out, I throw trash all over... all over the ring... And then, I start eating garbage.
jokes.punchline: Completely disgusting and inappropriate character concept
jokes.explanation: Iconic Frank moment - perfectly captures his gross-out humor and misunderstanding of entertainment

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: That's my character, I'm the Trash Man. I come out, I throw trash all over... all over the ring... And then, I start eating garbage.

jokes.text: A sixty year old man eating trash, who wants to see that?
jokes.punchline: Stating the obvious problem with Frank's plan
jokes.explanation: Good straight man reaction highlighting the absurdity

dialogue.line: A sixty year old man eating trash, who wants to see that?

jokes.text: How we like to protect our eggs from predators. And our young, we regurgitate to our... All the...
jokes.punchline: Literal bird behavior described as badass wrestling persona
jokes.explanation: Charlie's complete misunderstanding of what makes something intimidating

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: How we like to protect our eggs from predators. And our young, we regurgitate to our... All the...

jokes.text: Okay, well I was under the impression that we were presenting ourselves as bird men. Which, to me... Is infinitely cooler than just sorta being a bird.
jokes.punchline: Mac's earnest distinction between bird-men and birds
jokes.explanation: Mac trying to salvage their concept with pseudo-logical distinctions

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Okay, well I was under the impression that we were presenting ourselves as bird men. Which, to me... Is infinitely cooler than just sorta being a bird.

jokes.text: I dated a lot of disable men in my past, so... I enjoyed the power.
jokes.setup: Artemis agreeing to help with the soldier
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting to enjoying power over disabled men
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Artemis moment - reveals horrible motivations matter-of-factly

dialogue.speaker: Artemis
dialogue.line: I dated a lot of disable men in my past, so... I enjoyed the power.

jokes.text: Fine, then I'll be Desert Grape.
jokes.setup: Being told she looks like a grape
jokes.punchline: Immediately adapting the insult into her character name
jokes.explanation: Quick, clever adaptation showing Artemis's confidence

dialogue.speaker: Artemis
dialogue.line: Fine, then I'll be Desert Grape.

jokes.text: Hey, did he send you any dick pics. Because it could be a mess down there.
jokes.punchline: Blunt sexual speculation
jokes.explanation: Typical Artemis inappropriateness but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Artemis
dialogue.line: Hey, did he send you any dick pics. Because it could be a mess down there.

jokes.text: Oh, you're biting my lips.
jokes.setup: Ben meeting who he thinks is Desert Rose
jokes.punchline: Artemis being too aggressive in the romantic greeting
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy beat showing Artemis's lack of subtlety in romantic situations

dialogue.line: Oh, you're biting my lips.

jokes.text: Oh no, no I... I twisted my knee getting of a plane in Germany. I'm uh. I was just trying to stay off of it.
jokes.punchline: Mundane explanation for not being disabled
jokes.explanation: Deflates expectations in a realistic way

dialogue.speaker: Ben the Soldier
dialogue.line: Oh no, no I... I twisted my knee getting of a plane in Germany. I'm uh. I was just trying to stay off of it.

jokes.text: Oh, hit me with your best shot you stupid bitch!
jokes.punchline: Immediate aggressive escalation
jokes.explanation: Classic Artemis aggression, perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Artemis
dialogue.line: Oh, hit me with your best shot you stupid bitch!

jokes.text: Oh, you got kids Maniac? - Nah, not anymore.
jokes.punchline: Ominous 'not anymore' with no explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly ominous delivery that implies something terrible without saying it

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, you got kids Maniac?

dialogue.speaker: The Maniac
dialogue.line: Nah, not anymore.

jokes.text: Look at this dude, that's just a bucket of chestnuts. What, is he just foraging for his food?
jokes.punchline: Bucket of chestnuts as evidence of primitive lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Specific weird detail that builds the character's derangement

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Look at this dude, that's just a bucket of chestnuts. What, is he just foraging for his food?

jokes.text: No, he kept calling you the N word earlier. Every time you turned your back. He's like you stupid N, go get me grease.
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of racist behavior
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about casual racism, shows how terrible Maniac is

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: No, he kept calling you the N word earlier. Every time you turned your back. He's like you stupid N, go get me grease.

jokes.text: No, he kept calling you the N word earlier... He was calling me the N word. Every time you turned your back. He's like you stupid N, go get me grease.
jokes.punchline: Revealing his casual racism
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Da Maniac's complete unfitness as their wrestling partner

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No, he kept calling you the N word earlier...

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: He was calling me the N word. Every time you turned your back. He's like you stupid N, go get me grease.

jokes.text: I don't give a shit about the troops.
jokes.setup: Being asked to wrestle for the troops
jokes.punchline: Blunt rejection of patriotic appeal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cricket moment - completely undermines their patriotic premise

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: I don't give a shit about the troops.

jokes.text: I don't feel we're reading as eagles. No bro, we look like assholes. I'm getting more of a chicken vibe.
jokes.punchline: Honest assessment of their pathetic appearance
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment about their obvious failure

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I don't feel we're reading as eagles.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No bro, we look like assholes.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I'm getting more of a chicken vibe.

jokes.text: Maybe we can go with the chicken thing. - The chicken boys! - We're not changing the songs.
jokes.setup: Considering changing their bird identity again
jokes.punchline: Charlie's enthusiasm for yet another bird name
jokes.explanation: Continuing the running gag of constantly changing their bird identity

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Maybe we can go with the chicken thing.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: The chicken boys!

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We're not changing the songs.

jokes.text: Even with your f... I love you. - We love you too, man. - I don't love him. I don't love him at all
jokes.punchline: Mac's immediate honest rejection
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Charlie's politeness and Mac's honesty

dialogue.speaker: The Maniac
dialogue.line: Even with your f... I love you.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: We love you too, man.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I don't love him. I don't love him at all

jokes.text: Introducing America's most hated terrorist. The Taliban.
jokes.punchline: Presenting Cricket as 'The Taliban' to troops
jokes.explanation: Hilariously inappropriate for a troops benefit show

dialogue.speaker: Announcer
dialogue.line: Introducing America's most hated terrorist. The Taliban.

jokes.text: America sucks! The terrorists rule. Ah la la la!
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top terrorist persona at troops benefit
jokes.explanation: Appropriately offensive for the context but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: America sucks! The terrorists rule. Ah la la la!

jokes.text: The eagles are out of ... It flies through the night... Don't you mess with it's heads now.... Or you'll see a fight. Yes, we have feathers, ah! But the muscles are man.
jokes.punchline: Terrible lyrics about being bird-men
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier bird discussion, cringe performance works for sustained uncomfortable comedy

dialogue.line: The eagles are out of ... It flies through the night... Don't you mess with it's heads now.... Or you'll see a fight. Yes, we have feathers, ah! But the muscles are man.

jokes.text: They are not responding to the pageantry at all.
jokes.setup: Crowd's lack of response to their performance
jokes.punchline: Clinical assessment of their obvious failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis response - using fancy words to describe obvious failure

dialogue.line: They are not responding to the pageantry at all.

jokes.text: I'm your real Desert Rose. That strange woman you been hanging out with. She's an impostor. Baby... Yeah, bitch! She is a slut!
jokes.setup: Dee trying to win back the soldier
jokes.punchline: Immediately calling Artemis a slut on the microphone
jokes.explanation: Typical Dee escalation but predictable outcome

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I'm your real Desert Rose. That strange woman you been hanging out with. She's an impostor. Baby... Yeah, bitch! She is a slut!

jokes.text: Oh come on ref. Is that even legal? - I didn't see nothing.
jokes.setup: Cricket throwing sand in the ring
jokes.punchline: Dennis as corrupt referee
jokes.explanation: Good callback to wrestling tropes and Dennis's character

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh come on ref. Is that even legal?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I didn't see nothing.

jokes.text: The Trash Man!
jokes.punchline: Trash Man character finally revealed
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Frank's earlier setup, iconic moment

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: The Trash Man!

jokes.text: I must had an itch.
jokes.setup: Getting hit in head with trash can
jokes.punchline: Casual explanation for clearly serious injury
jokes.explanation: Good understatement of obvious trauma, fits Cricket's delusional nature

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: I must had an itch.

jokes.text: I knew this shit wasn't fake.
jokes.setup: Earlier argument about wrestling being fake
jokes.punchline: Charlie vindicated by actual violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to opening argument, Charlie was technically right

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I knew this shit wasn't fake.

tmdb_overview: The gang puts together a wrestling show for returning troops. Meanwhile, Dee finally meets the soldier that she has been communicating with over the internet.
